steps: Homogenise the tissue for 90 seconds at 20 Hz using a Mixer Mill or Tissue Lyser. 00:01:30

Prepare 300 µL lysis buffer per well. A B 1 Lysis solution 287 µL 2 RNase solution (10 mg/mL) 3 µL 3 Proteinase K solution (20 mg/mL) 10 µL 4 Total 300 µL Add the 300 µL lysis buffer to each well of tissue plate.
Spin the plate for 10 minutes at 1,000 RPM. 1000 rpm, 15 °C, 10 minutes

Seal the plate and mix on the plate shaker. Make sure the paste is resuspended. Incubate at 55°C for 150 minutes, shaking at 1000 rpm (or shake the plate for one minute every 30 minutes). 02:30:00 55 °C
Transfer 200 µL of lysate to a new extraction plate.
Dissect e.g. 8 drone heads for a single sample. Pool the dissected material into a 2 mL safe-lock tube. Add 4 3.2 mm stainless steel ball bearings and 500 µL PBS.
Centrifuge the homogenate for 10 minutes at 16,000 g and remove and discard the top layer (i.e. PBS). 16000 x g, 21 °C, 10 minutes
Transfer about 150 mg of the tissue paste to a 96-well plate for lysis. The remaining paste can be stored at -80°C and used for subsequent extractions.
Set up the reagents from the ZYMO Quick -DNA Magbead Plus Kit
Add the following reagents to the 7-position ReservoirRack %s %s

Run the epMotion protocol 88x Quick-DNA Magbead Plus premixed beads DNA rinse. Because the rinse step is programmed once for each plate column, the protocol has to be copied and modified for each run.

The master program is at Dearden/Tom/Quick-DNA Magbead Plus/88x Quick-DNA Magbead Plus premixed beads DNA rinse. Copy that file to your Runs directory and change the name. Use a name that includes today's date.
Change the sample number at steps 1 and 55. Delete rinse steps between 42 and 52, leaving only the steps for columns that have samples in them.
Run the protocol with level sensing enabled.
creator: Tom Harrop
